title: rubus × geniculatiformis
intro: Rubus × geniculatiformis is a hybrid species of the Rubus genus, resulting from the cross between the dewberry (Rubus argutus) and the bramble (Rubus caesius), and is commonly known as the dewberry bramble. This plant has been found to possess medicinal properties due to its high content of anthocyanins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Rubus × geniculatiformis include its potential anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to reduce the risk of chronic diseases such as cardiovascular disease, diabetes, and certain types of cancer. In herbal preparations, the leaves and fruits of Rubus × geniculatiformis can be used to make teas, infusions, and jams, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal and health benefits.
